name: review-heuristics
description: Heuristic review, design, and debugging across seven domains, routed by domain then intent then surface. DOMAINS: dx (APIs/SDKs/CLIs/docs/setup/errors/auth/telemetry developer experience); docs (documentation experience — README/quickstarts/API refs/help centers/llms.txt/agent-readable docs); perf (systems performance & observability — latency/SLOs/tracing/profiling/capacity); test (test-suite quality — unit/integration/e2e/property/contract/snapshot/mutation, flakiness, pruning); ux (user-facing product UX & accessibility — usability, forms, navigation, WCAG); ui-craft (visual UI design & polish — mockups, design systems, prototypes, motion, decks); architecture (clean architecture — dependency rule, layering, ports/adapters, DDD, bounded contexts). Pick the domain, then the skill routes intent (audit/design/debug/...) and surface, dispatches domain-specific reviewer lenses, scores findings, and emits an intent-specific report with optional tracking. Use ui-craft to CREATE/polish a UI and ux to AUDIT one. Grounded in canonical DX/UX/perf/test/architecture literature (per-source provenance below).

inspired_by.name: The Design of Everyday Things
inspired_by.author: Don Norman
inspired_by.contribution: Affordances, signifiers, slips-vs-mistakes error taxonomy; mental-model alignment.

inspired_by.name: 10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design
inspired_by.author: Jakob Nielsen
inspired_by.contribution: Heuristic-evaluation format; severity rubric; visibility-of-system-status and recognition-over-recall, adapted to developer surfaces.

inspired_by.name: How to Design a Good API and Why It Matters
inspired_by.author: Joshua Bloch
inspired_by.contribution: API design principles: if in doubt leave it out, naming, modules over methods, document everything.

inspired_by.name: A Philosophy of Software Design
inspired_by.author: John Ousterhout
inspired_by.contribution: Deep modules with simple interfaces; defining errors out of existence; comments-as-design.

inspired_by.name: The Twelve-Factor App
inspired_by.author: Adam Wiggins
inspired_by.contribution: Config in env, dev/prod parity, logs as event streams — operational DX baseline.

inspired_by.name: Command Line Interface Guidelines
inspired_by.author: clig.dev contributors
inspired_by.contribution: Concrete CLI conventions: help, flags, output, exit codes, TTY detection.

inspired_by.name: Observability Engineering
inspired_by.author: Charity Majors, Liz Fong-Jones, George Miranda
inspired_by.contribution: Debuggability as a first-class surface: high-cardinality context, traceability, sampling.

inspired_by.name: Accelerate
inspired_by.author: Nicole Forsgren, Jez Humble, Gene Kim
inspired_by.contribution: DORA metrics: lead time, deployment frequency, MTTR, change failure rate — make inner-loop quality measurable.

inspired_by.name: Stripe API Design Guidelines
inspired_by.author: Stripe
inspired_by.contribution: Public-facing reference for HTTP API conventions: versioning (date-pinned with response transforms), errors, pagination, idempotency keys, expansion, webhook signature verification.

inspired_by.name: Builders' Library — Timeouts, Retries, and Backoff with Jitter
inspired_by.author: AWS
inspired_by.contribution: Canonical retry design: exponential backoff with full jitter, per-client token-bucket retry budgets, server-directed retry timing — the load-bearing primitive behind any SDK that retries safely under load.

inspired_by.name: Smithy IDL specification
inspired_by.author: AWS
inspired_by.contribution: Open-source IDL formalizing SDK behavior traits: @idempotencyToken, @paginated, @retryable, @streaming. Drives auto-generated SDKs with consistent middleware, waiters, and event streams.

inspired_by.name: API Improvement Proposals (AIPs)
inspired_by.author: Google
inspired_by.contribution: Alternative API design philosophy to date-pinned versioning: path-based versions, opaque pagination tokens (AIP-158), request IDs for idempotency (AIP-155), long-running operations (AIP-151). The second canonical reference design.

inspired_by.name: Claude SDK and platform documentation
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: Primary source for AI-SDK patterns: per-content-block parameters (cache_control, citations), two-tier streaming with typed accumulators, opaque reasoning artifacts with continuity signatures, batch processing namespace, agent-SDK hooks.

inspired_by.name: Client SDK and Agents SDK documentation
inspired_by.author: OpenAI
inspired_by.contribution: Primary source for the typed exception hierarchy keyed to HTTP semantics, .parse() structured-output flow, agent loop with declarative stop conditions and handoffs, tracing-as-default with vendor-plugin export.

inspired_by.name: AI SDK
inspired_by.author: Vercel
inspired_by.contribution: Streaming-first AI/Agent SDK design: streamText / streamObject with partial output, tool definition from typed schemas (Zod), declarative stopWhen predicates, provider-agnostic dispatch via shared model interface.

inspired_by.name: Instructor
inspired_by.author: Jason Liu
inspired_by.contribution: Pioneered the patch-the-LLM-client pattern: response_model= with Pydantic validation, validation-error retry loops with the validator message fed back as semantic context (distinct from transport retries).

inspired_by.name: Pydantic AI
inspired_by.author: Samuel Colvin
inspired_by.contribution: Schema-first agent SDK: Agent(output_type=Model), @agent.tool with RunContext[Deps] dependency injection, semantic retries on validation failure carried into the agent loop.

inspired_by.name: What Color is Your Function?
inspired_by.author: Bob Nystrom
inspired_by.contribution: Async/sync function-color framing: SDKs that ship only one surface force consumers to thread-pool-wrap or run-loop-bridge at every callsite. The case for dual sync/async surfaces with identical method shapes.

inspired_by.name: Semantic Conventions
inspired_by.author: OpenTelemetry
inspired_by.contribution: HTTP and GenAI span-attribute conventions: http.request.method, http.response.status_code; gen_ai.operation.name (create_agent / invoke_agent / invoke_workflow), gen_ai.usage.{input,output}_tokens, gen_ai.response.finish_reasons, gen_ai.conversation.id for session correlation, and aggregated chat-history attributes (gen_ai.system_instructions, gen_ai.input.messages, gen_ai.output.messages) introduced in v1.37. SDKs depend on the OTel API (not the SDK) so downstream applications pick the exporter; experimental-status attributes are dual-emitted via OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN during migrations.

inspired_by.name: Agent Experience: How to design products that agents can actually use
inspired_by.author: WorkOS contributors
inspired_by.contribution: Names agent experience (AX) as a distinct design surface from DX: LLMs cannot read tooltips, recover from ambiguity, or maintain context across retries the way humans do; they operate at machine speed on surfaced text alone. Frames structured-error envelopes, idempotency keys, machine-readable interface contracts (OpenAPI, MCP), and structured-output return types as load-bearing for any SDK called by an agent.

inspired_by.name: Safe Observability: A Framework for Automated PII Redaction from LLM Prompts in OpenTelemetry Pipelines
inspired_by.author: International Journal of Computer contributors
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical finding that AI-system observability pipelines account for 25–40% of all discovered PII exposure in post-incident reviews because traces and error reports retain raw prompts and responses longer than the inference path and are read by more people. Motivates SDK-layer content-capture toggles (record_content) and pluggable redactors over downstream-collector-only redaction, and quantifies the trade-offs of co-located NER vs dedicated redaction tiers.

inspired_by.name: Hyrum's Law
inspired_by.author: Hyrum Wright
inspired_by.contribution: Every observable behavior of an interface is depended on. Frames stable-contract reasoning.

inspired_by.name: Application Security Verification Standard (ASVS)
inspired_by.author: OWASP Foundation
inspired_by.contribution: Standard checklist for authentication, session, and access control; concrete requirements per security level.

inspired_by.name: Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Lifecycle Management (SP 800-63B)
inspired_by.author: NIST
inspired_by.contribution: Authenticator assurance levels, lifecycle requirements, password and token handling rules.

inspired_by.name: Stripe API Key Management
inspired_by.author: Stripe
inspired_by.contribution: Operational patterns for token scoping, rotation, revocation UX, and test-vs-live key separation.

inspired_by.name: The Essence of Software
inspired_by.author: Daniel Jackson
inspired_by.contribution: Concept design: software is composed of concepts that interact; extensions have their own concept lifecycle.

inspired_by.name: Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software
inspired_by.author: Erich Gamma, Richard Helm, Ralph Johnson, John Vlissides
inspired_by.contribution: Named patterns for extension-point design: composite, observer, strategy applied to plugin contracts.

inspired_by.name: VS Code Extension API
inspired_by.author: Microsoft
inspired_by.contribution: Concrete patterns for declarative extension points, contribution points, activation events, and lifecycle.

inspired_by.name: Privacy by Design: The 7 Foundational Principles
inspired_by.author: Ann Cavoukian
inspired_by.contribution: Privacy by default; user control and visibility; embed privacy into design — operational frame for telemetry consent.

inspired_by.name: GDPR Article 7 — Conditions for Consent
inspired_by.author: European Parliament and Council
inspired_by.contribution: Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous; withdrawable. Frames telemetry opt-in requirements.

inspired_by.name: Diátaxis Documentation Framework
inspired_by.author: Daniele Procida
inspired_by.contribution: Four orthogonal documentation modes (tutorials, how-to guides, reference, explanation) that should never be mixed in one page.

inspired_by.name: Don't Make Me Think
inspired_by.author: Steve Krug
inspired_by.contribution: Users scan, don't read; F-pattern scanning; signposting; minimize cognitive load — applied to docs IA.

inspired_by.name: Language Server Protocol Specification
inspired_by.author: Microsoft
inspired_by.contribution: Uniform editor-integration contract: speak LSP and you get autocomplete, diagnostics, hover, and jump-to-def across modern editors.

inspired_by.name: The Pragmatic Programmer
inspired_by.author: Andrew Hunt, David Thomas
inspired_by.contribution: Power editing; tool fluency; the IDE is part of the SDK surface.

inspired_by.name: Systems Performance
inspired_by.author: Brendan Gregg
inspired_by.contribution: The USE method (utilization, saturation, errors); profile before optimizing; methodology for diagnosing performance problems.

inspired_by.name: Response Times: The 3 Important Limits
inspired_by.author: Jakob Nielsen
inspired_by.contribution: The 0.1s / 1s / 10s thresholds for user-perceived latency: instant feel, train-of-thought, attention loss.

inspired_by.name: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
inspired_by.author: Martin Kleppmann
inspired_by.contribution: Latency framing: percentile matters (p50 hides the tail); average vs p99; head-of-line blocking.

inspired_by.name: Producing Open Source Software
inspired_by.author: Karl Fogel
inspired_by.contribution: The README as the project's front door: name, value, install, first use, link out. Welcoming patterns for evaluators and contributors alike.

inspired_by.name: Open Source Guides
inspired_by.author: GitHub
inspired_by.contribution: Operational patterns for README, CONTRIBUTING, CODEOWNERS, issue and PR templates as surfaces that surface the right context at the right time.

inspired_by.name: Semantic Versioning 2.0.0
inspired_by.author: Tom Preston-Werner
inspired_by.contribution: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH contract: breaking changes bump MAJOR, additions bump MINOR, fixes bump PATCH. The version is a contract integrators rely on.

inspired_by.name: Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts (SLSA)
inspired_by.author: OpenSSF
inspired_by.contribution: Provenance, signed releases, two-factor publish, immutable version tags, reproducible builds as the baseline for any package other people install.

inspired_by.name: Stripe Samples
inspired_by.author: Stripe
inspired_by.contribution: Operational pattern for a samples surface: one repo per language, CI'd against the current SDK, runnable in five minutes, curated rather than community-dumped.

inspired_by.name: Keep a Changelog
inspired_by.author: Olivier Lacan
inspired_by.contribution: Chronological changelog with stable categories (Added/Changed/Deprecated/Removed/Fixed/Security), Unreleased section at the top, version-and-date heading per release.

inspired_by.name: AGENTS.md convention
inspired_by.author: agents.md contributors
inspired_by.contribution: A hand-curated repo-root file that coding agents read first to learn project context, commands, invariants, and load-bearing rules. The single source of truth across harnesses.

inspired_by.name: Model Context Protocol Specification
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: Uniform protocol for exposing tools, resources, and prompts to coding agents. Speaking MCP gets every conforming host the same capabilities without per-host plugins.

inspired_by.name: Evidence-Driven Agent Scaffolding
inspired_by.author: Mündler et al. (ETH Zürich / LogicStar.ai)
inspired_by.contribution: Scaffolding written from fewer than three observed failures produces plausible boilerplate that hurts agent success; rules must be promoted from a logged pattern, not invented.

inspired_by.name: Research Report — Effective Documentation Patterns and Practices for DX, AX, and UX
inspired_by.author: Informed Skills research synthesis
inspired_by.contribution: Primary synthesis for the skill's cross-audience frame: shared foundations, DX/UX/AX divergences, unique AX requirements, and conflict-resolution patterns.

inspired_by.name: The Documentation System
inspired_by.author: Daniele Procida
inspired_by.contribution: Four-mode documentation taxonomy and the anti-mixing rule for tutorials, how-to guides, reference, and explanation.

inspired_by.name: Docs as Code
inspired_by.author: Anne Gentle and Write the Docs contributors
inspired_by.contribution: Version control, PR review, automated tests, link checks, and CI as the operational baseline for durable documentation.

inspired_by.name: Information Architecture: For the Web and Beyond
inspired_by.author: Rosenfeld, Morville, and Arango
inspired_by.contribution: Organization, labeling, navigation, search, card sorting, and IA vocabulary for docs systems.

inspired_by.name: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2
inspired_by.author: World Wide Web Consortium
inspired_by.contribution: Accessibility floor for help and docs: focus visibility, target size, semantic structure, captions, transcripts, and consistent help.

inspired_by.name: Improving API Usability
inspired_by.author: Brad Myers and Jeffrey Stylos
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical grounding for treating examples as primary developer documentation rather than decorative supplements.

inspired_by.name: OpenAPI Specification 3.1.0
inspired_by.author: OpenAPI Initiative
inspired_by.contribution: Schema-as-docs model for HTTP APIs, including descriptions and examples that tools and agents consume directly.

inspired_by.name: MCP Tool Descriptions Are Smelly!
inspired_by.author: Md Hasan and coauthors
inspired_by.contribution: Evidence that tool-description quality changes agent task success and that more description can regress behavior when it is not targeted.

inspired_by.name: Agent Skills overview
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: Progressive disclosure model for agent-readable instructions: metadata always loaded, instructions on trigger, resources on demand.

inspired_by.name: Evaluating repository-level context files for coding agents
inspired_by.author: Gloaguen and coauthors
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical caution that maximalist always-loaded context can reduce task success and increase cost; grounds minimal, measured AX docs.

inspired_by.name: Help and Documentation
inspired_by.author: Nielsen Norman Group
inspired_by.contribution: Help in the moment of need, contextual help, and support for both search-first and browse-first help behavior.

inspired_by.name: Stripe API Errors reference
inspired_by.author: Stripe
inspired_by.contribution: Structured error envelope pattern: machine-readable code, user-facing message, offending parameter, and doc link.

inspired_by.name: Cloudflare documentation for AI tooling
inspired_by.author: Cloudflare
inspired_by.contribution: Markdown-for-agents, retrieval-friendly content, token budgeting, and content negotiation as a way to serve humans and agents from one source.

inspired_by.name: Systems Performance: Enterprise and the Cloud (2nd ed.)
inspired_by.author: Brendan Gregg
inspired_by.contribution: USE method (utilization, saturation, errors) as a systematic resource-bottleneck checklist; profile-before-optimize discipline; the symptom-to-root-cause methodology that anchors the resources, latency, throughput, and diagnose paths.

inspired_by.name: BPF Performance Tools: Linux System and Application Observability
inspired_by.author: Brendan Gregg
inspired_by.contribution: eBPF-era Linux observability primitives and the workflow of moving from coarse system metrics to fine-grained kernel/userspace tracing without restart or instrumentation overhead.

inspired_by.name: Thinking Methodically about Performance
inspired_by.author: Brendan Gregg
inspired_by.contribution: ACM Queue article that codified the USE method as a publishable methodology; the canonical citation behind the resources playbook's good-signals and common-failures lists.

inspired_by.name: Site Reliability Engineering: How Google Runs Production Systems
inspired_by.author: Betsy Beyer, Chris Jones, Jennifer Petoff, Niall Richard Murphy (eds.)
inspired_by.contribution: The Four Golden Signals (latency, traffic, errors, saturation); SLI / SLO / SLA framing; error-budget policy as the bridge between reliability and feature velocity. Anchors the metrics and slos playbooks.

inspired_by.name: The Site Reliability Workbook: Practical Ways to Implement SRE
inspired_by.author: Betsy Beyer, Niall Richard Murphy, David K. Rensin, Kent Kawahara, Stephen Thorne (eds.)
inspired_by.contribution: Worked examples of SLI selection, SLO target setting, and error-budget policy operationalization. The slos playbook's good-shaped patterns trace back to this volume.

inspired_by.name: Distributed Systems Observability: A Guide to Building Robust Systems
inspired_by.author: Cindy Sridharan
inspired_by.contribution: The three-pillars framing (logs, metrics, traces) along with its limits — pillars are emission shapes, not a complete observability theory. Anchors the cross-pillar reasoning in tracing, logs, and metrics playbooks.

inspired_by.name: Observability Engineering: Achieving Production Excellence
inspired_by.author: Charity Majors, Liz Fong-Jones, George Miranda
inspired_by.contribution: High-cardinality, structured-event observability and the critique of pre-aggregated metrics for novel-question debuggability. The tracing and logs playbooks lean on this for the diagnose path.

inspired_by.name: Guerrilla Capacity Planning: A Tactical Approach to Planning for Highly Scalable Applications and Services
inspired_by.author: Neil J. Gunther
inspired_by.contribution: Universal Scalability Law (USL) extending Amdahl with a coherency term, modeling the regime where adding nodes degrades throughput. Anchors the capacity-planner lens and the throughput playbook.

inspired_by.name: The Art of Computer Systems Performance Analysis: Techniques for Experimental Design, Measurement, Simulation, and Modeling
inspired_by.author: Raj Jain
inspired_by.contribution: Foundational treatment of experimental design, measurement statistics, queueing models, and common-mistake catalogues for performance studies. Anchors the verification / measurement-method discipline across all surfaces.

inspired_by.name: High Performance Browser Networking
inspired_by.author: Ilya Grigorik
inspired_by.contribution: Browser- and network-tier performance fundamentals: TCP/TLS handshake costs, HTTP/2 multiplexing, head-of-line blocking, critical rendering path. Anchors the browser-tier slice of the latency playbook.

inspired_by.name: High Performance Web Sites: Essential Knowledge for Front-End Engineers
inspired_by.author: Steve Souders
inspired_by.contribution: The original 14 front-end performance rules; bulk-of-display-time-is-client-side framing. Still cited in browser-tier audits despite age.

inspired_by.name: Dapper, a Large-Scale Distributed Systems Tracing Infrastructure
inspired_by.author: Benjamin H. Sigelman, Luiz Andre Barroso, Michael Burrows, Pat Stephenson, Manoj Plakal, Donald Beaver, Saul Jaspan, Chandan Shanbhag
inspired_by.contribution: The canonical distributed-tracing model: trace context propagation, span causality, sampling discipline, and the dependency graph as the unit of system understanding.

inspired_by.name: GenAI Semantic Conventions
inspired_by.author: OpenTelemetry
inspired_by.contribution: Institutional schema for tracing AI and agent workloads: gen_ai.operation.name (create_agent / invoke_agent / invoke_workflow), gen_ai.usage.{input,output}_tokens, gen_ai.response.finish_reasons, gen_ai.conversation.id for session correlation, and the v1.37 aggregated chat-history attributes (gen_ai.system_instructions, gen_ai.input.messages, gen_ai.output.messages). Status is Development with most attributes Experimental; dual-emission via OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN is the documented migration path. Vendor-specific extensions are accepted alongside the canonical attributes so downstream backends do not require translation.

inspired_by.name: The Tail at Scale
inspired_by.author: Jeffrey Dean, Luiz Andre Barroso
inspired_by.contribution: Why tail-tolerant designs (hedged requests, tied requests, micro-partitioning, selective replication) are load-bearing at scale, and why the median is an actively misleading reliability signal.

inspired_by.name: A Proof for the Queuing Formula: L = lambda * W
inspired_by.author: John D. C. Little
inspired_by.contribution: Little's Law — the foundational identity relating concurrency, throughput, and latency that constrains every closed-loop perf analysis.

inspired_by.name: The RED Method: How to Instrument Your Services
inspired_by.author: Tom Wilkie
inspired_by.contribution: Rate / Errors / Duration triplet for service-level instrumentation; complements the resource-oriented USE method. Anchors the service-tier metric set in the metrics playbook.

inspired_by.name: How NOT to Measure Latency
inspired_by.author: Gil Tene
inspired_by.contribution: Coordinated omission and the HdrHistogram approach to lossless latency recording. Anchors the latency playbook's measurement-method discipline and the metrics playbook's percentile-aggregation pitfalls.

inspired_by.name: SQL Performance Explained: Everything Developers Need to Know about SQL Performance
inspired_by.author: Markus Winand
inspired_by.contribution: Index-anatomy-first treatment of SQL performance: B-tree internals, leaf-node traversal, predicate selectivity, function-wrapped column traps. Anchors the DB-tier slice of the latency and resources playbooks.

inspired_by.name: xUnit Test Patterns: Refactoring Test Code
inspired_by.author: Gerard Meszaros
inspired_by.contribution: The canonical test-smell catalog and the test-fixture / object-mother / test-data-builder vocabulary used across the unit, integration, snapshot, and failure-modes references.

inspired_by.name: Working Effectively with Legacy Code
inspired_by.author: Michael Feathers
inspired_by.contribution: Characterization tests, seams, and the test-as-pin-down technique. Anchors the characterization-purpose framing across unit and integration playbooks.

inspired_by.name: Test-Driven Development By Example
inspired_by.author: Kent Beck
inspired_by.contribution: Spec-purpose framing, the red/green/refactor cadence, and the discipline of writing the test that drives the design.

inspired_by.name: Unit Testing: Principles, Practices, and Patterns
inspired_by.author: Vladimir Khorikov
inspired_by.contribution: The four-quadrant test-value model: protection-against-regressions x resistance-to-refactoring x fast-feedback x maintainability. Anchors the false-pass mode and the score rubric's robustness dimension.

inspired_by.name: Explore It! Reduce Risk and Increase Confidence with Exploratory Testing
inspired_by.author: Elisabeth Hendrickson
inspired_by.contribution: Charter-driven exploratory testing, session-based test management, and the role of automated tests as scaffolding for exploration.

inspired_by.name: Rapid Software Testing methodology
inspired_by.author: James Bach and Michael Bolton
inspired_by.contribution: The SFDIPOT mnemonic for testable aspects and the FEW HICCUPPS consistency oracles, distilled into the oracles core reference.

inspired_by.name: QuickCheck: A Lightweight Tool for Random Testing of Haskell Programs
inspired_by.author: John Hughes and Koen Claessen
inspired_by.contribution: The original property-based testing framework and the shrinking idea - failures shrink to a minimal counterexample.

inspired_by.name: Hypothesis design rationale
inspired_by.author: David R. MacIver
inspired_by.contribution: Practical generator design, choosing properties, and the integration of property-based testing into mainstream test suites.

inspired_by.name: Agile Testing: A Practical Guide for Testers and Agile Teams
inspired_by.author: Lisa Crispin and Janet Gregory
inspired_by.contribution: The agile testing quadrants and the practical framing of test-strategy decisions across layers.

inspired_by.name: Succeeding with Agile
inspired_by.author: Mike Cohn
inspired_by.contribution: The original test pyramid framing - bias toward fast unit tests at the base, fewer integration tests, fewest UI tests at the top.

inspired_by.name: The Testing Trophy
inspired_by.author: Kent C. Dodds
inspired_by.contribution: An alternative to the pyramid emphasizing integration tests as the highest-value layer for typical web apps; informs the strategy playbook's discussion of layer investment.

inspired_by.name: The Practical Test Pyramid
inspired_by.author: Ham Vocke
inspired_by.contribution: Concrete practical layer definitions and the often-quoted critique that pyramid is more shape-suggestion than rule.

inspired_by.name: Approval Testing
inspired_by.author: Llewellyn Falco
inspired_by.contribution: The approval-testing pattern (snapshot-style review of generated output) and the discipline of human review of every diff.

inspired_by.name: An Analysis and Survey of the Development of Mutation Testing
inspired_by.author: Yue Jia and Mark Harman
inspired_by.contribution: Comprehensive survey of mutation testing techniques, equivalent-mutant problem, and operator selection strategies.

inspired_by.name: Are Mutants a Valid Substitute for Real Faults in Software Testing?
inspired_by.author: Rene Just
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical evidence that mutation score correlates with real-fault detection, validating mutation testing as a meaningful signal.

inspired_by.name: Consumer-Driven Contracts: A Service Evolution Pattern
inspired_by.author: Ian Robinson
inspired_by.contribution: The CDC pattern - consumers state what they actually use; providers verify that subset. Foundation of modern contract testing.

inspired_by.name: Pact Foundation documentation
inspired_by.author: Pact Foundation contributors
inspired_by.contribution: Practical patterns for broker-published pacts, provider-side verification, and integrating contract tests into CI.

inspired_by.name: An Empirical Analysis of Flaky Tests
inspired_by.author: Qingzhou Luo
inspired_by.contribution: Categorization of flakiness root causes (async wait, ordering, network, time, concurrency) used in the failure-modes file.

inspired_by.name: Flaky Tests at Google and How We Mitigate Them
inspired_by.author: John Micco
inspired_by.contribution: Industrial-scale flakiness mitigation: quarantine, ownership, deflake-or-delete deadlines.

inspired_by.name: Systems Performance: Enterprise and the Cloud
inspired_by.author: Brendan Gregg
inspired_by.contribution: The USE method (utilization, saturation, errors) and the discipline of profiling before optimizing. Anchors the performance playbook's choice-of-what-to-measure framing.

inspired_by.name: On the Criteria To Be Used in Decomposing Systems into Modules
inspired_by.author: David Parnas
inspired_by.contribution: Information hiding as the basis of module decomposition; 'what should be hidden' as the criterion that predates and underwrites every later layering scheme.

inspired_by.name: Hexagonal Architecture
inspired_by.author: Alistair Cockburn
inspired_by.contribution: Ports and adapters; the application has interior ports and exterior adapters that translate to/from external technologies. Symmetric inbound/outbound framing.

inspired_by.name: The Onion Architecture
inspired_by.author: Jeffrey Palermo
inspired_by.contribution: Concentric rings; outer rings depend on inner rings, never the reverse. Dependency-rule expressed as geometry.

inspired_by.name: Agile Software Development: Principles, Patterns, and Practices
inspired_by.author: Robert C. Martin
inspired_by.contribution: SOLID; package cohesion (REP, CCP, CRP) and package coupling (ADP, SDP, SAP) principles. Foundational frame for the dependency rule.

inspired_by.name: Clean Architecture: A Craftsman's Guide to Software Structure and Design
inspired_by.author: Robert C. Martin
inspired_by.contribution: The dependency rule as the single load-bearing invariant; concentric layers (entities, use cases, interface adapters, frameworks & drivers).

inspired_by.name: Domain-Driven Design: Tackling Complexity in the Heart of Software
inspired_by.author: Eric Evans
inspired_by.contribution: Ubiquitous language; entities, value objects, aggregates, domain services, repositories; bounded contexts and context mapping.

inspired_by.name: Implementing Domain-Driven Design
inspired_by.author: Vaughn Vernon
inspired_by.contribution: Pragmatic tactical-DDD detail: aggregate-design rules, eventual consistency between aggregates, integration patterns between contexts.

inspired_by.name: Learning Domain-Driven Design
inspired_by.author: Vlad Khononov
inspired_by.contribution: Modern DDD framing; subdomain types (core, supporting, generic) inform context boundaries and integration patterns.

inspired_by.name: AnemicDomainModel
inspired_by.author: Martin Fowler
inspired_by.contribution: Names the anti-pattern of domain objects that hold only data while logic lives in service classes — undercuts the entire point of object-oriented domain modeling.

inspired_by.name: BoundedContext
inspired_by.author: Martin Fowler
inspired_by.contribution: Compact restatement of bounded context: a model is valid only within its context; cross-context translation is explicit.

inspired_by.name: Patterns of Enterprise Application Architecture
inspired_by.author: Martin Fowler
inspired_by.contribution: Layered architecture; Repository, Unit of Work, Data Mapper, Service Layer patterns. The catalog underwriting most adapter-side mechanics.

inspired_by.name: Enterprise Integration Patterns
inspired_by.author: Gregor Hohpe and Bobby Woolf
inspired_by.contribution: Messaging patterns; integration between bounded contexts via channels, translators, and routers rather than shared databases.

inspired_by.name: Monolith to Microservices
inspired_by.author: Sam Newman
inspired_by.contribution: Strangler-fig, branch-by-abstraction, and other sequenced patterns for extracting bounded contexts without big-bang rewrites.

inspired_by.name: Building Microservices
inspired_by.author: Sam Newman
inspired_by.contribution: Service decomposition aligned to bounded contexts; coupling-vs-cohesion trade-offs for context boundaries.

inspired_by.name: Flux: An Application Architecture for React
inspired_by.author: Facebook
inspired_by.contribution: Unidirectional data flow: actions → dispatcher → stores → views. Frontend analog of the dependency rule.

inspired_by.name: The Elm Architecture
inspired_by.author: Evan Czaplicki
inspired_by.contribution: Model-update-view with pure functions and explicit side-effect handling. Frontend grounding for layered boundaries and effect isolation.

inspired_by.name: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2
inspired_by.author: W3C Web Accessibility Initiative
inspired_by.contribution: Shared accessibility baseline for perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust web content; used for accessibility findings and verification.

inspired_by.name: WCAG 2.2 is a Web Standard W3C Recommendation
inspired_by.author: W3C Web Accessibility Initiative
inspired_by.contribution: Confirms WCAG 2.2 Recommendation status and frames it as the current stable WCAG 2 standard family baseline.

inspired_by.name: A Web for Everyone
inspired_by.author: Sarah Horton and Whitney Quesenbery
inspired_by.contribution: Inclusive design patterns and personas that keep accessibility tied to real tasks instead of a checklist-only review.

inspired_by.name: Forms that Work
inspired_by.author: Caroline Jarrett and Gerry Gaffney
inspired_by.contribution: Form design principles: ask only what is needed, make labels and examples clear, reduce mode errors, and support correction.

inspired_by.name: Claude design workflows source pack
inspired_by.author: Project-local source notes
inspired_by.contribution: Seven-stage design workflow, focused question rounds, visual craft rules, component conventions, output-format choices, motion patterns, and anti-slop review heuristics.

inspired_by.name: Claude design host integration source pack
inspired_by.author: Project-local source notes
inspired_by.contribution: Artifact-host protocols for tweak panels, fixed-canvas scaling, speaker notes, direct edit, mentioned elements, bundling, export, and handoff.

inspired_by.name: GOV.UK Design System
inspired_by.author: GOV.UK Design System team
inspired_by.contribution: Component and pattern guidance with coded examples; reinforces system-first composition and task-specific patterns.

inspired_by.name: Human Interface Guidelines
inspired_by.author: Apple
inspired_by.contribution: Visual hierarchy, platform consistency, layout adaptation, and motion guidance for polished interface behavior.

inspired_by.name: Material Design 3 Foundations
inspired_by.author: Google
inspired_by.contribution: Foundational vocabulary for color, typography, motion, interaction states, and component-system consistency.
